title: Graveyard Shift Takeover on Established Day-Only Account
narrative: A cardholder with 18+ months of transaction history exclusively between 8am-6pm local time suddenly executes 3-7 transactions between 3:00-5:30am, typically starting with a $15-45 test charge followed by escalating amounts ($180, $340, $620). The account shows no prior activity in txn_hour bins 3-5, making the overnight window a hard boundary violation. Fraudsters exploit credential theft during victim sleep hours, knowing detection lag gives them a 4-6 hour window before account holder wakes. Transactions often target digital goods (ProductCD='W') or international MCCs with instant fulfillment. The addr1 may remain consistent while dist1 jumps to 800+ miles, suggesting VPN use to mask new geolocation while maintaining some address fingerprint continuity. Card1_txn_count_24hr spikes from baseline of 1-2 to 5-8 transactions.
indicators: Zero prior transactions in txn_hour range 3-5am across 18+ month history, then 3-7 overnight charges
Transaction sequence: $15-45 initial test, followed by $180+, $340+, $620+ within 90 minutes
card1_txn_count_24hr increases from 1-2 baseline to 5-8 during overnight window
dist1 distance jumps to 800+ miles while addr1 hash remains unchanged
All overnight transactions target ProductCD 'W' or MCCs 5816, 5818, 5967 (digital/subscription)
is_night flag=1 for all suspicious transactions versus 0% historical night activity
card1_amt_zscore exceeds 3.5 standard deviations from cardholder's historical daytime pattern
summary: An account that has only transacted between 8am-6pm for over 18 months suddenly shows 3-7 charges between 3:00-5:30am, starting with a small test charge then rapidly escalating to $600+, often targeting digital goods while the distance spikes 800+ miles but the billing address stays the same.
reasoning: Fraudsters use stolen credentials during the victim's sleep hours to exploit the 4-6 hour detection window before the cardholder wakes, testing the card with a small charge then quickly draining it on instantly-fulfilled digital products before the account can be frozen.
indicator_explanations: Account that never transacts at night suddenly becomes active in early morning hours
Classic testing pattern escalates from small probe to increasingly larger fraudulent charges
Card velocity spikes dramatically during overnight window versus normal daily usage
Purchases originate from distant location while billing address stays the same
Fraudster targets easy-to-monetize digital goods and subscription services exclusively
Every suspicious transaction occurs during hours the cardholder has never shopped
Purchase amounts are drastically higher than what this card spends during daytime
